text:
Once upon a time, a magical forest breathed life into everything it touched. In the heart of the forest lived an old owl. She knew everything about her enchanted home.

One quiet morning, after a series of thunderstorms had filled her glasses with sparkling droplets, she noticed something strange. Birds were chirping. Flowers weren't opening. The canopy above hadn't parted for clouds. Yet something was different.

Curious, the owl fluttered down from her perch and inspected the world.

In the tree lined streets, gardeners were surprised to find hummingbirds dancing.

Down by the river, fish were singing.

Even squirrels were hopping joyfully.

With an insatiable curiosity, the wise owl embarked on a journey. She discovered that a forgotten spell had been cast centuries ago. All living things had forgotten their music.

She flew through the woods, guided by the melodies she heard. Each creature remembered its song; it was just suppressed by the spell. The owl pieced it together.
